desc:
The plain slopes slightly as it approaches the hill just north of you. Strange
patterns appear and disappear in the grasses as winds blow in gusts around the
hill. The vegetation is not very high or dense here, but further away you can
see that it grows thicker and higher. The hill blocks your view north, but in
all other directions you see the endless plains stretching out towards the
horizon.
name: A Mildly Slanting Plain

desc:
Here the grasses are waist high, thick and dense, being the perfect environment
for smaller animals to hide and thrive. The height of the grass makes it behave
like waves rolling on the sea, shifting even at the slightest gust of wind. High
up in the sky you can see a few small, dark dots, swirling and twisting about in
strange patterns.
name: The Long Flowing Grass

desc:
The green flowing grass here resembles an ocean, waves rolling over its surface
as the wind blows. The way it stretches out endlessly towards the horizon makes
the similarity even more striking. Not much can be seen that breaks the surface
of the plains, and nothing disturbs the peace except for an occasional bird or
animal that scurries about in the undergrowth.
name: The Flowing Plains

desc:
Waving in the wind, the grasses stand proud and tall here, covering the ground
completely. As you look at the scenery, you notice that the appearances of the
various grasses change to your west, as the grasses become shorter, thinner and
bleaker. A bit to your north you notice a hill rising in the plains.
name: The Flowing Plains

desc:
The ground of these plains is cracked and dry. Nothing grows here and the few
turfs of grass that remain are yellow and completely dead. Dust rises as a gust
of wind blows by, slowly falling back, covering almost everything with a thin
layer of brown. The brown colours seem to prevail no matter where you look, but
suddenly you catch a glimpse of some greenery far to the east.
name: The Dead Plains

desc:
The plains rise sharply just to your north, rising to become a high and steep
hill. In the otherwise featureless land, this hill is an excellent landmark,
being visible from afar. Around you in all other directions the plains flow on
as lush as ever. Something is strange about the colour to your west though, as
it seems to be more bleak and yellow than green.
name: Plains below a Hill

desc:
The grass lies flat on the ground here, as if something has weighed it down for
some time and was just recently removed. The remains of an old campfire can be
spotted as well, the blackened pieces of wood scattered about on the ground. The
surrounding high grass has hidden the old campsite from view rather well, as it
is thick and almost man-high.
name: An Old Campfire

desc:
Now and again, a small gust of wind lifts the dust from the ground, forming a
small hazy veil that for a second prevents you from seeing what lies ahead of
you. After it slowly falls to the ground, you can see that most of the grass
here has withered to dust and not many things live in the area. The ground
starts to become green due east, but as you look farther west, you cannot see
anything living, not even grass.
name: Withered Plains

desc:
The plains here seem drained, tired and lifeless. Void of anything that walks or
crawls, the only things living here are a few sturdy grasses and some small
weird-looking plants. Although the land seems alive and vibrant just north and
east of here, the area south and west seems to suffer from some sort of plague
that keeps the land in eternal pain and misery.
name: Withered Plains

desc:
This area of the grassland stands sheltered by a higher plateau to the east.
Without the constant assault of the chill prevailing winds a rich little meadow
has sprung up here. The grass is particularly long and thick, and more bushes
grow here than anywhere for miles around. Even a lone elm tree stands proudly
here, ancient and tall.
name: A Sheltered Meadow

desc:
North and west the land seems blighted and this part of the landscape has not
escaped the effect entirely. The grass underfoot is limp and rather dry.
Yellow-green in colour, none of the vegetation looks healthy, but a few coarse
weeds hold their heads up defiantly. To the east the picture brightens, where a
sea of lush growth can be seen.
name: A Dying Plain

desc:
Whatever has blighted the lands to the west and north of here, this part of the
plains seems to have escaped its touch. Perhaps the sheltering influence of the
high plateau to the east has played a role. The grass here grows tall and dark,
and numerous small shrubs have sprouted up amid the green blades.
name: Beneath an Overlook

desc:
Many heavy feet have trod the ground here and what little vegetation there was
has been reduced to a muddy pulp. A stiff wind howls across this high plateau,
made icy in the distant heights of the Misty Mountains. Not far to the west and
north the highlands come to an abrupt end. To the south the high ground gives
way more gradually, where a more gentle slope offers access to the low-lying
plains beyond.
name: On a High Plateau

desc:
East and north the ground climbs toward a vast plateau which stretches toward
the foothills of the Misty Mountains in the east. South and west it falls off
gently toward a lower-lying plain, lush with deep green grass. Despite the slope
a good deal of vegetation can be seen here, from thick tufts of grass to coarse
weeds and even a few rather small shrubs.
name: A Steeply Sloping Plain

desc:
Sloping gently down to the west, this land marks the eastern boundary of a vast
area of rolling plains. Thick, green grass fights for supremacy over the coarse
tufts of yellow grasses, which are dominant on the higher lands to the east. A
few stunted bushes have also made their home here, clinging to life despite the
gradient. Someone or something has recently uprooted many of them, although the
reason for this destruction is not apparent.
name: Rising Plain

desc:
The land slopes very gradually upward to the east, climbing toward a higher,
more exposed portion of the plains. In the shelter of this plateau the land here
is relatively protected from the prevailing winds, and the grass grows tall and
strong. Thick bushes are dotted across the landscape and the ground is peppered
by wildflowers.
name: On a Grassy Slope

desc:
Not far to the northeast the land begins a gentle ascent towards a higher
plateau. In all other directions the ground is quite flat, stretching out far
into the distance. All that strives to grow here must do so despite the constant
buffeting by the winds which howl across the open moors. Low tufts of coarse,
yellow-green grass form a chaotic patchwork with thick clumps of purple heather,
but no taller plants are to be seen here.
name: Open Moors

desc:
This is a transitional area. Westward a vast, gently rolling plain opens out
before you covered by a thick blanket of dark green grasses. Here the ground
rises gently, climbing toward a higher plateau, where the only plant life is the
coarse, yellow grass characteristic of peaty moorland. Both types of vegetation
vie for control of this area and despite the slope it seems that the more
verdant, lowland species are going to win out.
name: A Rising Plain

desc:
To the south the land slopes gently toward a small stream. East it climbs to a
high and bleak plateau which stands in stark contrast to the lush plains you can
see below you to the west. A variety of plant life makes its home on these
slopes, but the dominant feature is coarse, rather yellow grass which grows in
thick clumps all about you.
name: Sloping Ground

desc:
Barely climbing above the wetlands to the east, and marking the crest of a
longer rise to the south, the land here slopes only a little. The earth is
blanketed by a patchwork of coarse, yellow-green grasses and dark purple
heather. Here and there the vegetation is interrupted by splotches of open
ground where water pools.
name: On a Heather-clad Rise

desc:
A small stream flows by, its muddy waters sometimes washing over its banks to
create what is almost a marshland. The lush, green grasses of the plains below
give way to wetland species here, with tall reeds and cattails proliferating.
This wet shoulder of land stretches south, following the course of the brook,
born and nurtured by its ever-present waters.
name: A Wet Shoulder

desc:
Twisting and turning, this shallow stream makes its haphazard way across the
high plains. Cutting its channel readily through the peaty ground it picks up a
lot of the red-brown dirt as it travels sluggishly along, turning here from a
westerly to a southerly course.
name: A Meandering Stream

desc:
Shallow and sluggish, this stream makes only slow progress as it wanders across
the high plains. It gathers a little more speed as it flows onward to the south
where the land gradually begins to fall away, losing altitude with each mile it
travels towards the valley floor southeast of here.
name: On a Shallow Stream

desc:
The stream gathers speed here as it traces the descending landscape. Resuming a
westerly course it chases down a gentle grade, frequently undercutting its own
banks and picking up more of the red-brown earth which has already given its
waters a rusty hue.
name: Following a Meandering Stream

desc:
Turning here from a westerly to a southerly course, the stream has begun to
undercut its own low banks. Already tainted by red-brown earth, the waters now
become even more turbid as they flow onward, picking up more of the soft dirt as
they go.
name: A Turbid Tributary

desc:
Two lively streams meet here forming a single larger waterway. Where the waters
mingle you can see a swirling pattern, for the slower stream from the north
bears heavy traces of red-brown earth whilst the more lively southern waterway
runs quite clear. Flowing together the water cascades westward, tumbling over
rocks and boulders as it makes its way along the valley floor.
name: Confluence of Two Streams

desc:
Chilly water cascades down from above, pouring into this basin from the east.
Following the contours of the land it then traces a channel to the north from
here, then onward down the length of the valley, racing westward toward an
eventual confluence with the Bruinen. A number of large boulders break through
the surface of the pool and the waters roil and play about them casting a thin
veil of mist high into the air.
name: At the Bottom of a Waterfall

desc:
Through the years this lively stream has cut a deep but fairly narrow channel
into the living rock. Following the slope of the land it races west from here,
cleaving a path around a massive granite prominence before dancing over the lip
of rock to tumble to the valley floor below. Swirling on a few boulders which
stand proud of the chilly surface the water splashes merrily and a fine mist
cools and dampens the air.
name: A Lively Stream

desc:
Ambling across this relatively flat countryside the waters of two streams have
combined to form a small river. Its bed is wide here, held in to the north by a
soft, low, peaty bank, whose earth is readily washed away by the gentle current.
South the terrain is rockier and slopes more steeply upward.
name: A Wide River

desc:
Wetland species dominate the landscape. A small stream flows by to the east,
merging with another waterway then flowing sedately past immediately to the
south of here. The low banks have often been breached by the waters, leaving
this area more like a marsh or mud flat than a shoreline.
name: On the Mud Flats

desc:
The ground slopes here, descending both to the south and to the east. Climbing
to the north it rises above the river valley to an area of open moorland. To the
east the descent is much more gradual, so that this sloping ground barely stands
above the muddy wetlands there. Lush green grass carpets the earth, and a few
small shrubs are dotted across the landscape.
name: A Grassy Incline

desc:
Though the stream which flows by to the east is small and quite sluggish its
waters have nevertheless breached its banks here often enough to leave this an
area of marshes and mud flats. To the west the landscape is dominated by flowing
grassy plains which give way, in the distance, to a rockier valley but here it
is the plants of the shallows that proliferate.
name: The Wetlands

desc:
Extending westward from the base of a small waterfall, a narrow ribbon of land
stretches along this side of the river. A thin veil of mist hangs in the air,
feeding the moss which blankets the cliff face to the south. Though the soil is
rich, fed by the stream-water, it is also rather marshy, and under the shade of
two looming cliffs the plant life here is rather poorer than might have been
expected.
name: On a Narrow Ribbon of Land

desc:
A small and cold chamber in the wall of the canyon, this rough cave is a work of
haste and need. The walls are rough, the floor is covered by stones and dust and
there is nothing in the room except a roughly made bed.
name: Sleeping Chamber

desc:
This small cavern is cut out of the rock of the canyon wall. The walls are
roughly cut, obviously in haste. The room is void of decorations and furniture.
In the eastern wall there is a small opening, leading into a seemingly small
chamber.
name: Small Cavern

desc:
This lone mound of granite is an extension of the high plains and hilly ranges
to the east and south of here, although it actually stands rather higher than
any of the nearby ground. This lofty eminence affords a good view of the
immediate area. A long valley stretches off to the east. South your view is
blocked by a high cliff, a part of the rapidly rising foothills and westernmost
ranges of the Misty Mountains. To the north a vast, rolling plain dominates the
scenery.
name: On a Rocky Prominence

desc:
Just north of here a small river cleaves a narrow channel through the rock.
South a massive cliff rears above you, just a small part of the rising foothills
of the mighty Misty Mountains. Under its sheltering shadow this small plateau is
afforded a measure of protection from the prevailing winds. This, together with
the ample supply of fresh water, has allowed a surprising variety and quantity
of vegetation to flourish here, from small shrubs and dense weeds to a single
small fir tree which stands not far from the water's edge, with its lowest
branches trailing close to the ground.
name: By a Rocky Rill

desc:
A fine mist rises from the south, climbing up past the top of the cliff towards
you. A gentle roar of water greets your ears. Though the ground here is quite
rocky, it bears a surprisingly rich amount of vegetation, perhaps due to the
sheltering influence of the cliffs which rear above you to the south. East of
here the greenery grows even thicker.
name: Above a Waterfall

desc:
A narrow plateau stands above the valley floor, sheltered to the south by steep
cliffs. Westward the land slopes gently downward but to the north the drop-off
is so sheer as to prevent travel in that direction. The vegetation here is
surprisingly rich, but seems oddly flattened in places, as if it had regularly
been trodden down by heavy feet.
name: Above the Valley Floor

desc:
This cave was most likely formed by water draining through the small cracks in
the roof and flowing downwards. To the north, where the floor stands lower, it
has formed a pool which fills that part of the cave entirely. Though it is
possible to move in that direction it would be necessary to swim in order to
reach the mouth of the cave.
name: A Damp Cavern

desc:
A narrow, turning tunnel links two larger caves. Its walls are rough and come
quite close together in places but the ceiling remains high, opening up even
more both west and north of here. The steady sound of dripping water can be
heard echoing about you, and the air feels quite chilly.
name: A Turning Tunnel

desc:
The chamber is littered with bones of small animals. While it is a mystery as to
how these creatures found their way into the cave it appears that they did not
meet their doom naturally, for almost all are broken into many pieces. Though
the chamber is fairly cramped movement is possible in three distinct directions.
name: A Rough Cavern

desc:
The rough floor of this cave slopes downward to the east and the roof also rises
in that direction. Thus, as you near the western extremity of the chamber, the
roof and floor draw ever closer together until they almost meet. It is here that
a large, deep crack can be seen in the rocky roof.
name: In a Low-roofed, Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Though this cave appears to be a natural one with rough, untooled walls and a
number of small stalactites hanging from the roof it is apparent that now it is
occupied by someone or something. Animal bones lie scattered on the floor and
the southern wall bears what can only be described as a door, crafted from a
single massive block of stone. A similar attempt to block the wide mouth of the
cave to the east was less successful, and three massive, shattered, rocky slabs
lie at the entrance.
name: In a Natural Cave

desc:
Here must be where the trolls come to rest during the hours of daylight. Furs
and skins of various creatures, big and small, are scattered in rough piles all
around. None have been tanned and all are in varying states of decay, a partial
explanation for the odour of decay which fills the air.
name: A Rough-hewn Chamber

desc:
Cold stone walls close in around you on all sides and the only apparent exit
from this chamber is through a square opening in the northern wall. Should this
somehow become blocked the only escape would be death. Apparently this is a
storage chamber for those treasures which the trolls steal from their hapless
victims. Much that is here seems to be worthless garbage, testimony to the
intelligence of the trolls, and of those who would risk their lives attempting
to purloin their hoard.
name: The Treasure Cave

desc:
The floor of this rough cave slopes downward to the east, deeper into the cold
rock of the hills. The air is chilly and dry, but tainted by an odour of decay.
A few bones lie scattered about the floor among the rotting remains of any
number of perishable items that are now too decomposed to be identified. Wood,
leather and cloth are all present among the garbage, but there is no indication
that anything of value remains.
name: A Large and Gloomy Cavern

desc:
A black tent has been hastily erected on this windy plateau. All vegetation has
been trampled and destroyed, and the ground is littered with all manner of
animal remains. Together these have formed an unsavoury morass which makes your
footing slippery and leaves a foetid odour which even the brisk breeze cannot
completely dispel.
name: At a Crude Encampment

desc:
The roof of the tent dips low to one side, suggesting that it is either in a
poor state of repair or has been erected carelessly. The ground upon which it
stands has been trampled and despoiled, but a few badly-tanned furs, which have
been tossed into one corner, offer a place to rest. The bones of small game
animals are scattered about, stripped of even the smallest shred of meat.
name: Inside a Tent

desc:
Regular gusts of frigid air buffet everything atop this bluff. This is the most
western margin of a vast plateau which stretches eastward to the very foothills
of the Misty Mountains. Standing at some height above the lower-lying plains to
the west this promontory has a bleak aspect, and only the most stubborn, low-
growing vegetation clings to life here.
name: At the Edge of a Plateau

desc:
You find yourself inside a fairly narrow tunnel. The walls high above you appear
natural, but closer to floor level show signs of working. Rough chisel marks
scar the cold, grey rock, speaking to the effort that must have been expended to
enlarge a smaller, natural crevice. A crude stair ascends the northern wall
giving access to a large stone trapdoor in the roof. The floor slopes downward
to the east as the tunnel widens and approaches a larger cavern.
name: A Rough-hewn Tunnel

desc:
Where the tunnel to the west appears crudely made, the craftsmanship in this
chamber is of a higher calibre. The walls describe a near-perfect circle and are
quite smooth. Strange runes cover their surface from floor to eye level and high
upon the wall, immediately facing the entrance, is painted the image of a huge,
lidless, red eye. Above your head a domed ceiling has been carved, and its
surface bears a thick coating of oily soot, as if attesting that many fleshy
pyres have been lit in this place.
name: A Domed Chamber

desc:
With the exception of the narrow exit to the north this chamber contains no
distinguishable markings. The walls are smooth, with small patches of moss and
the distinct stains of water. A soft breeze can be felt through a number of
small cracks in the roof.
name: Back Chamber

desc:
The cavern ceiling and walls here have been blackened with fire. On these walls,
hundreds of skeletons have been hung with ropes and chains. The white skeletons
provide a stark contrast against the cavern. The floor is covered in a thick
layer of dust and random bones that have fallen from the skeletons.
name: Rough Cavern

desc:
The tunnel bends sharply here. Rough rocks underfoot cause the ground to be
uneven and difficult to navigate in the dark. Faint echoes coming from somewhere
seem to curl their way around the corners of the bend, or perhaps they are just
imaginary.
name: Bend in a Dark Tunnel

desc:
A thick layer of dust lies mostly undisturbed on the tunnel floor. Though
somewhat cramped, there is a little standing room in this tunnel. Some old
cobwebs hang down from the ceiling above and stretch over rocks. The molted
carapace of a large spider can be seen dangling from the web.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here is a tight fit as it bends, but opens up into a small pocket
cave allowing anyone to stand comfortably again. It is very dark in here with
small insects creeping along underfoot. Some stale air floats in from around the
bend of the tunnel from time to time.
name: Bend in a Dark Tunnel

desc:
A small terminating tunnel branch can be seen to the side of this larger tunnel,
creating a tiny nook. The nook is extremely small and cramped, offering barely
enough room for even a hobbit to stretch freely. The air within the darkness of
the tunnel is very still, with no noise to be heard.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The main tunnel bends and breaks off into tiny little side passages that lead
into their own unreflective walls of darkness. The sound of bats can be heard
echoing from the tiny passages alongside faint wafts of stale air. The walls of
the tunnel are hard and grey in colour.
name: Bend in a Dark Tunnel

desc:
The walls are composed of what appears to be grey stone. The passage continues
on, hard grey rock in every direction. The layers of sediment from which the
rock had formed show a history of the passage of time. The rocky walls echo even
the smallest of sounds.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The darkness inhabits this tunnel like a feral animal in a den. The black stone
walls reflect little if any light and the rough, textured, rocky floor aids in
that cause. The ceiling of the tunnel is not very high, making the black walls
seem closer than they are.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
The stone which makes up the walls here is ancient but extremely solid. The
walls smoothly curve up to the pinnacle of the cave, creating a domed ceiling.
In contrast to the walls, the floor underfoot is quite rough, with jagged stones
and uneven surfaces abounding.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Here an intersection of tunnels offers several paths forward. The walls are
quite smooth and composed of a grey stone. On the walls, near the entrance of
each tunnel, symbols have been scrawled in black charcoal. They gleam in the
darkness.
name: Tunnel Junction

desc:
The area here behind this crack is quite concealed from the area above from
which a draft can be felt. A few insects creep along the ground and up the
walls. To the south, the walls of the crack narrow in closely as they lead into
a tunnel which prevents anyone from seeing past into the darkness beyond.
name: Behind a Crack

desc:
Heaps upon heaps of bones litter the cave with only a clear path down the
middle. The path seems to be set above the rest of the ground, as if the bones
were in a pit of lower rock. Insects crawl over ribs, climb into eye sockets,
and scuttle along spines.
name: Bone-filled Cavern

desc:
The cavern's rock is of a monotone grey colour. Stones have been cleared along
the floor to create an easy path. Dust lies thickly outside the lines but the
way forward is very clear. The cavern opens up to a hill towards the west.
name: Dusty Cavern

desc:
The hills give way slightly here opening up into a small cleft. Sheltered from
the elements a few green patches of shrubbery have sprouted here, but the ground
is still mostly composed of dirt and rock. The sound of the wind blowing through
the hills can be heard from time to time.
name: Cleft in the Hills

desc:
The hills rise and fall like waves in an ocean that has been frozen in time. The
topsoil being almost entirely dirt, the slopes of the hills are smooth and
mostly featureless. A gash runs between two of the hills making for somewhat of
a path between them.
name: Low Foothills

desc:
The tunnel travels through the surrounding rock in a straight manner. The smooth
walls seem to be composed of a grey stone that is moist to the touch. Streaks of
different coloured sediments can be seen in its layers. The rock is not
particularly hard here and crumbles easily.
name: Damp Tunnel

desc:
The ceiling of this tunnel is quite low. There is one very large hole in the
wall, as if chunks of the stone have been taken out and moved somewhere else.
The ground is covered in mushrooms and moist, crumbly rocks. The tunnel becomes
progressively wetter as it heads westward.
name: Damp Tunnel

desc:
The small tunnel bends here abruptly. Stones line the border of the floor, some
with long slash marks along them as if they were used for sharpening something.
The cracks are stuffed full of soft wet fungus, keeping the air moist. The
tunnel gets more dry towards the east but wetter to the south.
name: Damp Tunnel

desc:
The sound of dripping water can be heard echoing through the cave. A small pool
of rather clear water has formed in a small basin in the rocks. A few mushrooms
have grown up the walls and around the small water sink. A musty smell pervades
the room.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
A heavy stone grate stands half-submerged in a pool of dirty water. Behind the
grate not much can be seen, but the bilge water that flows through it is rank
and cloudy. Around the edges of the pool, the corpses of small rodent-like
animals lie in the murk. Mushrooms grow everywhere on the walls.
name: At a Stone Grate

desc:
A long, grey slab of stone lies along one side of the area. Various insects
crawl along the edges, occasionally disappearing underneath it. On the face of
the slab, a roughly-drawn face appears to have been etched into the stone and
filled in with shavings of a white rock.
name: At a Stone Slab

desc:
The stone walls expand giving birth to a wide, spacious cavern. Though the stone
in the walls is of a dark grey colour mixed with speckles of black and red,
white stones are strewn across the floor. A cold draft and the sound of water
can be heard raining down from a large hole in the ceiling.
name: Wide Cavern

desc:
Tunnels converge here, forking in different directions. At the centre of the
intersection, a pile of stones has been placed with a log from a tree sticking
up in the middle. On the log, arrows have been scratched pointing in all
directions into the tunnels, making the path through the area no clearer.
name: Tunnel Complex

desc:
Several low boulders with flattened tops have been placed around this cave. In
the centre of the cavern, a space has been cleared and a fire pit has been dug.
Charred and blackened rocks line the edge of the pit and inside the pit, two
iron posts have been stuck with a horizontal iron rod spanning across them.
name: Troll Kitchen

desc:
This massive cavern stretches into the darkness, its walls unable to be seen.
The floor is quite smooth and level save for a single crack. The crack, if it
can truly be called a crack, yawns open like a horrible mouth in which nothing
can be seen except the void.
name: Expansive Cavern

desc:
The back wall of this cave has several heavy, iron shackles that have been
wrapped around boulders. The shackles are bent and warped as if something has
writhed and strained against them. A firepit, still warm to the touth, sits in
the centre of the room. A primitive iron rod rests next to the firepit with some
charred flesh seared onto it.
name: Wall of Crude Chains

desc:
Several tunnels converge in front of a huge arch that is blocked by a stone gate
topped with skulls. A moat filled with dusty stones and bones lies in front of
the gate. A cold draft can be felt from the eastern tunnel where a huge slab of
rock has been placed.
name: At the Troll Gate

desc:
What appears to be a stone house has been built in the corner of this cave out
of a heaping pile of rock. A slab of splintered wood has been pulled across the
door frame, and short pillars, somewhat sculpted into crude shapes, lie out
front. The centre of the house has a charred fire pit that has been crudely
constructed out of a circle of rocks.
name: Troll Home

desc:
The air here is cold and dry and the walls are made of a slick stone. Claw marks
have been raked down the sides of the walls as if something has tried to dig
around. Skulls and other various bones litter the ground. A low mist hovers over
the stone floor before disappearing into a deep pit.
name: Above a Dark Pit

desc:
A low ceiling covers the cave, the colour of the stone weaving in and out of
blues, greys, and blacks. Huge stone slabs have been set side by side on the
floor of the cavern, the slabs being a couple of feet high. Old raggedy furs and
skins are heaped on each of the slabs, like sheets on hard beds.
name: Enormous Troll Home

desc:
Ragged skins have been crudely stitched together forming a stinking awning for
this abode. A stone bed lies in the back corner of the hovel with a pile of
dirty, torn blankets at its foot. A stone slab has been placed here with several
carvings depicting crude scenes on its flat surface.
name: Dirty Troll Hovel

desc:
The slick stone here bears claw marks that lead westward alongside huge piles of
refuse, bones, and rubble. It seems like there was a cave-in to the west of this
deep pit that was quickly dug out and enlargened. The rubble-strewn passage has
now been cleared allowing easy access. Some foul mist clings to the walls before
dispersing as the pit opens to the west into a tunnel.
name: Dark Pit

desc:
This very wide tunnel bears the marks of crude tools used to chisel out the
passage. The tunnel descends downwards into the dark and rises on its opposite
side to the east where it terminates rather abruptly in a rubble-covered
cave-in. Some of the rubble has been removed allowing easy passage through.
name: Long Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Movement here is tricky due to the piles of jagged and jumbled sheets of rock
that look to have been haphazardly thrown down from above through a craggy
opening in the ceiling. Enough rocks have piled up to form a sort of stairway
leading up into a foul-smelling darkness.
name: Jumbled Tunnel

desc:
This large cavern is dominated by a sizeable waterfall loudly dashing down the
north wall. Black liquid courses along water-smoothed rock before vanishing into
dark fissures while the spray makes the air cool and wet. A well-trodden pathway
leads to a western glow while to the east it vanishes into a deep, darkness.
name: Dark Waterfall

desc:
Phosphorescent lichen clings to the walls and ceiling of this cavern causing a
wash of dim glow all over. The hard stone path leading east and west has been
scraped clean of any growth as evidenced by large and small clawmarks. A
rushing, roaring sound can be heard to the east, and a cooler draft comes from
there.
name: Glowing Tunnel

desc:
Weak torchlight flickers along the northern and southern walls of this high
east-west tunnel. Some large boulders block parts of the tunnel at intervals,
casting dark shadows along the way and lending the tunnel a very ominous
atmosphere. A very faint roaring noise comes from somewhere to the east.
name: Ominous Tunnel

desc:
This cavern's ceiling is so high it cannot be seen in the darkness. Around the
walls are dim torches striving to light this vast space. On the west end, a
stairway has been carved into the rock, vanishing upwards. Dominating the
southern wall is a massive stone door, carved with foul glyphs. The floor is
strewn with old debris and broken weaponry; evidence of battles long ago or
perhaps just abandoned junk.
name: Lofty Cavern

desc:
This low, vaulted tunnel snakes its way north and south. The chipped ceilings
and walls show signs of crude toolwork. To the north, a heavy stonedoor rests,
engraved with foul glyphs and a leering goblin with twisted eyes and lolling
tongue. On the west wall, a smaller, dark stonedoor is outlined with skulls of
various sizes. The handle looks like bone.
name: Low Roofed Tunnel

desc:
The floor of this cavern is worn smooth and has probably seen much use to reach
its current state. The long passage slopes between two openings; the one higher
up is shrouded in darkness while the lower one seems lit by the same scattered
torches on the walls that sets this cavern in a dim, flickering glow.
name: Long Slope

desc:
A series of sharply sloping caves run from the surface down deeper into the
roots of the Misty Mountains. The caves shown signs of being roughly hewn in
places, apparently to widen them and ease travel for large bipeds of unknown
origin.
name: Sloping Caves

desc:
Softly glowing moss clings gingerly to glistening stalagmites along the weaving
stone pathway. Above, luminescent lichen tattoos the various stalactites
protruding from the damp cavern ceiling. Dripping water echoes throughout the
chamber. A glowing path slopes down into darkness.
name: Glowing Cavern

desc:
This high vaulted cavern spans hundreds of yards in every direction. Stalagmites
and stalactites spring from floor and ceiling, and a quiet, dark pool of still
water lies in the centre of the cavern. The walls of this cave are blanketed
with brightly glowing green mosses, and beds of the same moss cover the slick,
wet ground.
name: Luminous Cave

desc:
A dark tunnel with arched ceilings snakes its way to the north and south amongst
rubble and debris. Some tiny stalactites can be see hanging from the ceiling on
the right side of the tunnel where water is slowly seeping out of the
surrounding rocks. The muddy walls of the tunnel show signs of the crude chisels
that were used to expand it.
name: Low Roofed Tunnel

desc:
This chamber is lined with bulbous rock walls worn smooth by countless years of
dripping condensation. Small droplets cling to every available surface. To the
east, a crude outline of a door is the only break in the otherwise solid stone.
An awful stench wafts in from the south, indicating something vile lies that
way.
name: Dark Dungeon

desc:
The slick walls of this chamber are marred only by the massive iron rings set
into the rock. Each is as wide as a horse, and likely almost as heavy. Their
only use seems to be as an anchor to chain up some immense beasts. The iron is
covered in thick scratches, apparently the result of the prisoners attempts to
gnaw their way free.
name: Dank Dungeon

desc:
A pile of every sort of horror covers this corner of the cavern. Rotten food,
old shoes, and every other sort of refuse are cemented together by noxious paste
of excrement. An occasion foot or finger can be seen mixed in with the rest the
filth. The air is thick with a ghastly stench that threatens to turn even the
strongest of stomachs.
name: Rotten Refuse Heap

desc:
The floor of the cavern gradually descends into a watery pit in the southwest
corner. The water covering that corner is black as pitch, covered in bubbles as
large as a man's head. It is entirely opaque, providing ample covering for
whatever vile creature might be lurking below.
name: Wet Pit

desc:
The eastern side of this low-roofed tunnel is covered in stalactites and
stalagmites that were formed by water slowly seeping out of the surrounding
rock. Natural mineral pigments, ranging in colour from ochre to yellow, can be
seen on the tunnel's walls as it snakes its way to the north and south.
name: Low-roofed Tunnel

desc:
An old mine was operating here, as evidenced by a large pile of rubble and some
heavy pine timbers that have been set into the tunnel as braces. This low
vaulted tunnel can be seen snaking its way north and south. The old mine leads
upwards into a dark cavern accompanied by a slight draft.
name: Low Roofed Tunnel

desc:
Ensconced torches line the walls of this cavern, casting dancing shadows along
the ceiling. The wide floor slopes downward toward a tunnel from which the
continuous rushing sound of water emanates. Tunnels lead to the north and west
into darkness.
name: Torchlit Cavern

desc:
A crudely made stairwell ascends at the western end of the tunnel accompanied by
a foul draft. The tunnel has otherwise been shaped by crude tools into a
corridor that would allow two men to pass side by side. The eastern end of the
tunnel expands out into a cavern.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
The air smells thickly of soil in this smooth, round tunnel. The walls are cut
sharp, but still made of natural strata, giving them a dubious sturdiness. The
floor has been flattened to allow travel for two and crunches underfoot with
fresh, sharply cut gravel. Half-way through, the tunnel makes a bend.
name: Bend in a Tunnel

desc:
Roughly hewn walls of stone line this deep, round tunnel. The dull grey, damp
stone is scored with harsh slashing marks indicating the strength of the hewer
and the hardness of the tool. The tunnel is straight and the floor of the tunnel
is flat, giving proof of the workmanship.
name: Deep Stone-walled Tunnel

desc:
Deep underground this tunnel winds its way through the heart of the earth. No
life is here, only clay besotten mire interworked with layers of hard packed
gravel. The tunnel is truly that, rounded and cut straight through; obviously
not a natural formation.
name: Deep, Dark Tunnel

desc:
Walls of rock and dirt form a deep tunnel underground, the sides hewn by hasty
but experienced miners. A dark band of thick gravel cuts diagonally through the
rest, smelling bitter and oily to the touch. The air is thick with cold, wet air
and dampens any sound.
name: Deep, Dark Tunnel

desc:
Dampness hangs in the air of this chilly, round tunnel. The walls are dirt and
rocks and although not completely smooth, the tunnel itself is true. The floor
of the tunnel is composed of grey and black crushed rocks, and is rather fresh.
name: Deep, Dark Tunnel

desc:
A round tunnel burrows through the rock and earth, leading eastwards so
straightly that it would make a fletcher proud. At its western end, the tunnel
ends in a short slope leading upwards into darkness. Most of the rock is dark
grey granite, with faint veins of worthless quartz glittering in places.
name: Tunnel in the Deep Earth

desc:
Long wisps of hair-filled goo hang from the roof, swinging in frequency with any
disturbance in the air. Some strands hang low enough to touch the ground,
creating a gooey trap for any insects that venture into its path. This inlet
continues to the west, and the ground grows red and black to the north, as
gravel intermixes with clay.
name: Muculent Cavern

desc:
Short, mucous-laden stalactites hang across the roof, slowly dripping their
contents onto the sticky floor below. The skeletal remains of rats, lizards and
insects lay imbedded in the thick goo, creating a jagged collection of white and
green. This gummy cavern extends to the east, and south, while to the north, the
ground becomes rocky.
name: Muculent Cavern

desc:
Several small depressions in the rocky ground have collected the mucous
surrounding them, creating green pools of slime, and death traps for any animals
that venture into them. Several small bodies lay in the stagnant pools, creating
a furry texture to the otherwise bland, green liquid. The slime-filled cave
continues to the north.
name: Muculent Cavern

desc:
The sides of this sinkhole are naturally jagged, and protruding rocks provide
easy footholds for travellers. Red and green mosses form a carpeted surface for
many of the steps, and the undersides of some host a variety of mushrooms and
insects. A warm, damp breeze can be felt occasionally from above, and the
squeaking of rats, and chittering of insects, echoes throughout the hole.
name: Tunnel in the Deep Earth

desc:
Below, a large, smooth sinkhole drops into the deep recesses of the earth. The
rocky ceiling above is shaped like a dome, and at its apex, a few long
stalactites grow from eons of accumulated moisture and sediment. The rocky cave
extends westard, into the dark interior of the mountain.
name: Round Grotto

desc:
A stone-walled tunnel passes through deep underground, the walls crushingly
strong and imposing. The mining work was quick, but skilled, leaving the path
straight through. The walls are a dreary grey, broken up by dull, rust-coloured
rivulets, dripping slowly down.
name: Deep Stone-walled Tunnel

desc:
The floor of this tunnel slopes slightly downward from east to west. The passage
is increasingly covered in dust and small stones. The tunnel seems to be very
old and to the east heavy timbers support the ceiling.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A swiftly flowing torrent, dark and freezing, carries from the south to the
north through a large, water-worn opening in a slate-grey wall before vanishing
into unknowable depths. A crude path leading up has been hewn out of the bare
stone on the western rockface.
name: Freezing Torrent

desc:
A small natural stair leads up into this noisy grotto. The source of the noise
is a a swift subterranean river that can be seen below; its dark grey waters
swirling, rushing, and clamouring. The grotto is incredibly wet with moisture
dripping from the ceiling and misting up from the rushing river current.
name: Noisy Grotto

desc:
A large hall with walls made out of stone blocks connects a purpose-built tunnel
to the north with a masterfully crafted hallway to the south. To the east two
dwarven statues crafted out of black stone stand tall crossing their hammers.
Between them there is an entrance leading deeper in what appears to be an old
tomb.
name: Large Hall

desc:
The walls of this tunnel are formed of robust stone blocks. However, to the
north a cave-in makes further passage impossible. It seems the tunnel was used
to haul heavy materials toward the south. To the east the cave-in left a big
hole in the wall leading into darkness. To the south the tunnel connects with a
large hall. The sound of water can be heard from a large hole in the ground.
name: Caved-in Tunnel

desc:
A huge natural cavern extends to the north while to the west there is a big hole
leading to what seems to be an artificial tunnel which was exposed after a
cave-in. Guano covers the floor suggesting bats living inside the cavern.
name: Natural Cavern

desc:
Towering above are two dwarven statues, one to the south and the other to the
north. To the east is a passage leading to a stairway what seems to be a tomb.
The passage is mosaicked with small polished stones creating a beautiful pattern
leading toward the stairway.
name: Passage into a Tomb

desc:
The stairway made of white marble stone descends deeper under the ground toward
the east. The walls are mosaicked with small polished stones creating a
beautiful pattern. On the top of the stairs there is a passage leading into a
larger hall.
name: Mosaicked Stairway

desc:
The white walls of this antechamber give it a simple yet beautiful look. The
only way out of the chamber seems to be toward the west where a stairway
ascends. The walls of the antechamber are not decorated in any way and only its
architectural features give a feeling for the size of this chamber.
name: Antechamber in a Tomb

desc:
A large stone sarcophagus is standing on the floor in the middle of the alcove.
Behind the sarcophagus toward the wall of the alcove is a white statue of a male
dwarven warden. The walls behind the statue are covered with decayed tapestries.
name: Northern Alcove

desc:
A small terrace stands out like an island in a sea of darkness that makes up
this pit. Peering into the dark depths, a faint glow of red can be seen. A
lengthy stone slab, scorched black from the heat and fumes that rise from below,
gives passage from the terrace to a tunnel in the west. A cold draft can be felt
to the east.
name: Black Pit

desc:
A dim red light creeps out from cracks in the floor far below; heat radiating
like an oven. The hazy glow flows past the rocks, casting shadows that dance and
shiver on the walls in a haze. A bubbling and popping sound joins the show,
acting as music in the darkness.
name: Smoking Chamber

desc:
The walls of this area are made of what appears to be pure obsidian. They gleam
in the smallest of light, capturing it in their smoky crystals. The rocks on the
floor are also made of obsidian, their edges jagged and sharp as razors.
name: Rising Chamber

desc:
A lake of darkness extends off to the side of this cave. A stone ledge borders
the bottomless hole, winding its path into the next cavern. The darkness inside
the abyss is thick and impossible to penetrate with any amount of light. A cold
draft can be felt leading down from further up in the chamber.
name: Rising Chamber

desc:
Heaps of useless junk are piled here and there, booty after a troll's heart. The
cave is quite dirty with objects lying about seemingly without rhyme or reason.
The only clear walkway is an aisle down the centre of the hoard.
name: Troll Hoard

desc:
Tall, rough walls of granite flank the centre of this cavern. The ceiling is
high above with long, needle-like stalactites reaching down. One particularly
large stalactite stretches all the way from the ceiling to the floor, acting
like a great pillar in the centre of the cave.
name: Granite Cavern

desc:
A small circular cavern is here, ringed with three large pillars. The pillars
stand like trees that have grown from the stone ground itself. Each stone has
some sort of painting drawn on it. The pillars are arranged equidistant from
each other forming a large triangle. On the ceiling, a great, black, lidless eye
stares down.
name: Circular Hollow

desc:
Huge, troll-shaped pillars the size of small towers support the cave ceiling
upon their sloping shoulders. In the centre of the cavern stands a throne that
seems to have been naturally-formed from the very stones themselves. The throne
sits atop a dais of black rock that protrudes from the cavern floor.
name: Natural Throne

desc:
A long black rock cuts through the chamber, dividing most of it in two. The
walls curve up to the ceiling and down the other side in an almost perfect half
moon. The floor is composed of ground-up obsidian and the temperature here is
very chilly. A cold draft can be felt coming in from somewhere above.
name: Chilly Chamber

desc:
A small cleft in the mountainside offers protection from the elements. High in
the mountains, not much can be seen on the slopes other than ice and stone. A
large protruding rock juts out of the back wall of the alcove. The back of the
alcove is strangely devoid of snow and ice.
name: Mountain Alcove

desc:
A narrow gap snakes its way through the mountains. There seems to be a faint
path which has been traced along the low slopes offering a way forward. Large
boulders have come to rest here, fallen from untold heights. The wind howls
through the gap.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
The natural stone walls of this low gorge are glazed in ice and locked in
permafrost. What perhaps was once a stream is now a frozen road of ice swishing
through the gorge. The snow on the ground is dotted with small holes.
name: Mountain Gorge

desc:
Two tall mountains stand like impossibly tall spires flanking the area.
Underfoot a deep snowdrift covers the ground and no sort of living plant life
can be seen. However, some sort of path, perhaps an animal track, seems to have
been patted into the snow.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
A mixture of dirt and snow covers the hard ground. The slopes are not too steep
here but higher up the mountains it's clear that the incline grows
significantly. A few boulders and stones which line the mountains offer some
hand-holds if necessary.
name: Mountain Pass

desc:
A rope bridge spans the distance across a gap between two mountain ridges. The
bridge dances in the wind, the ropes holding it together more like bars of solid
ice. The distance between the two peaks is vast but nothing compared to the
dizzying fall to the ground below.
name: Rope Bridge

desc:
The sounds of nature echo about this stone-clad area, muffled only slightly by
the copious amounts of snow. What appears to have once been a small pond lies in
the centre of a flat part of the mountain slope but it has long frozen over.
name: Mountain Slope

desc:
Far up above, tall mountain peaks pierce the sky, their silhouettes like
white-tipped arrow heads. The slopes from the mountains slant sharply downwards.
Large boulders are dotted here and there in the white snow like speckles on
marble.
name: Between the Mountains

desc:
A huge rock juts out of the side of the mountain extending into nothingness. The
ground is a dizzying distance below and the view offered by this proud perch is
unmatched. The wind howls and moans around the rock almost in protest of this
scar drawn across the sky.
name: Natural Bridge to Nowhere

desc:
A crude shaft has been dug here which seems to be a very old mining operation.
Heavy timbers support the walls and ceiling at regular intervals, giving off a
heavy smell of pine tar. The shaft generally runs from west to east, narrowing
the deeper it gets. Fissures in the stone create intricate patterns of smaller
cracks crisscrossing larger cracks of varying sizes.
name: Crude Mining Shaft

desc:
A narrow mining shaft winds its way through the stone here. Severe cave-ins show
instability in the mining shaft. Several marble stone blocks indicate there was
some artificial structure to the south, however rubble now completely blocks it
and makes further passage impossible. The eastern wall looks quite unstable with
lots of rubble and natural stone blocks in that direction.
name: Narrow Mining Shaft

desc:
Harshly chopped slag forms a round tunnel through the deep. Grey stone is rough
to the touch and shows scores from the wickedly tough tool that made the work.
The air is thick with moisture, and covers all with a cold, damp sweat. Under
foot, the stone has been made flat and allows for travel of two wide.
name: Deep Stone-walled Tunnel

desc:
Strong and proficient work has made a straight, rough tunnel through the hard
granite. The ceiling here is low and drips rust-coloured water in echoing
splashes. The walls are dark grey, interworked with shiny, white courses and
small green spots.
name: Low-ceilinged Tunnel

desc:
A dwarf high tunnel runs through here, straight and rough. The stone walls are
damp, dripping slowly from the moisture in the air of the deep underground, and
shine black. Nary a sound can be heard in the lifeless dark, the ground being
smooth, flat shale.
name: Low-ceilinged Tunnel

desc:
An adeptly mined tunnel has been carved through dirt and rocks deep underground.
The air is loamy and wet, yet more clay smelling than dirt. The tunnel is
straight and sturdy, and the ground is packed hard with crushed, yellow stone.
name: Low-ceilinged Tunnel

desc:
A tunnel ends in a dark, cramped cavern, the walls natural and pitted. The floor
and ceiling are crammed with small, shiny stalagmites and stalactites, some of
which reach from top to bottom. The stone is black and ancient, covered in wet
mire and slick to the touch.
name: Dark, Cramped Cavern

desc:
Into the west, north, and east walls, dark recesses have been chipped until a
full-size goblin could fit inside. It looks as if many hundreds of goblins could
sleep in here at the same time. The dank musty odours of goblin breath and body
stench fill the air.
name: Goblin Lodge

desc:
A huge natural cavern extends to the south. Big stalactites hang from the
ceiling and some broken pieces scattered over the cavern floor suggest some
stalactites came down. The western side of the cavern is covered with rubble
from several cave-ins.
name: Natural Cavern

desc:
Loose stones and boulders lie everywhere, ready to be sent tumbling and sliding
down the mountain at the slightest provocation. The ground rises up to a rocky
outcropping to reveal a protected cave leading down. Long sticks and branches,
the wreckage of some huge bird's nest, can be found lying in the cave.
name: Steep Slope
